label: agentdb • import a 500-row CSV in one host-mediated call
query: There is a file named commits.csv in your working folder with 500 commit records (columns: sha, author, additions, deletions). Load it into your agent database as a table named commits, then tell me exactly how many rows the table has.
notes: WS1 proof: a 500-row file must land via the host db_import path (one tool call, no row data through tokens), NOT a db_insert loop. mustNotCallTools pins that the model didn't fall back to per-row inserts; dbState reads the isolated per-eval-agent DB AFTER the run (500 rows, SUM(additions)=125250 from the deterministic fixture). maxToolCalls is far below 500, so a row-by-row strategy can't pass.
